trusted_baseline.commit_semantics: verified live main head after PR #27 merged with only the first remediation commit; live Git determines the current head

Refresh project state after governance merges

workstreams.write_owner: ChatGPT conversation: ServiceTracer — Governed Azure Operations Lab
workstreams.scope: Repair the partial PR #27 merge and address the second operations-and-recovery review within the existing six-file scope. Permitted paths: infra/replacement/validate_execution_design.py, infra/tests/test_collector_replacement_execution_design.py, docs/designs/collector-replacement-execution.md, docs/reviews/collector-replacement-rollback-decision-2026-07-21.md, .project/active-work.json, and .project/handoffs/current-state.md. Protected paths: infra/replacement/collector-replacement-contract.json, .github/workflows/**, infra/modules/**, application source, credentials, and Azure mutation scripts.
workstreams.next_gate: Run fresh CI on the new code-bearing remediation head, then obtain another operations-and-recovery review while PR #28 remains draft.

known_open_pull_requests.title: feat: scaffold Azure network foundation
known_open_pull_requests.action: Do not treat as current implementation work. Review for unique durable evidence, then close as superseded through a separate explicit action.

known_open_pull_requests.title: Complete collector rollback review remediation
known_open_pull_requests.action: Patch the three second-review findings, run fresh exact-head CI, and obtain another independent operations-and-recovery review. Do not authorize or perform Azure mutations.

reviewed_coordination_head.authority: live GitHub checks and review evidence; this coordination-only head must not be described by the predecessor run

verified_gates: complete Python unit suite including 23 focused recovery-remediation tests
operational evidence collection and CLI smoke test
ci_verification.claim_boundary: Both recorded runs prove repository consistency only. They do not prove Azure snapshot recoverability, Trusted Launch bootability, rollback, cost, or execution authority. A new code-bearing remediation head requires fresh CI.

required_patch: validate the exact ordered quiesce sequence and require stopping the collector service
require rehearsal VM deallocation and temporary-compute removal before replacement compute while retaining only approved recovery artifacts
record final-head CI and review authority without presenting predecessor CI as final-head evidence
operations_and_recovery.claim_boundary: This is a repository review decision. It does not authorize Azure authentication, mutation, replacement, rehearsal, or rollback.

conditions: fresh authenticated subscription-specific meter, SKU-availability, and quota preflight before any execution
stop and obtain renewed cost approval if projected temporary cost exceeds CAD 4
unconditional stop if projected temporary cost exceeds CAD 10
cleanup owner and deadline must be approved before any temporary resource is created
azure_cost.claim_boundary: Planning feasibility only; this is not an actual-cost observation, subscription-specific quotation, budget approval, or Azure execution authorization.

required_gates: exact ordered quiesce actions including collector-service stop
rehearsal compute deallocation and temporary VM/NIC removal before replacement compute
negative tests for action reordering, service-stop omission, cleanup omission, and compute overlap
fresh exact-head CI and another operations-and-recovery review
next_bounded_operation.next_gate: Complete only the six declared remediation files, run fresh CI on the new code-bearing head, and return that exact head for another operations-and-recovery review. Do not merge, authenticate to Azure, or perform mutations.
